lead_list.notes: ANZ dataset link is an internal product page, not a verifiable external source; plausible but unverified record count.

action_plan.notes: TechVisa.com.au existence unverified; Westpac hardship-notice angle is strong but Cassini stack detail unconfirmed.

swot_analysis.notes: Generic SaaS SWOT points; APRA alignment claim lacks cited standard; opportunities repeat Stone & Chalk from action_plan.

first_customers.notes: Cassini and AEM stack attributions presented as fact without source citations; hardship-notice signal for Westpac is plausible but unhedged.

events_resources.notes: All event links are internal relative URLs with no dates or confirmed venues; cannot verify these events exist as described.

setup_compliance.notes: Strongest grounded section; 43.5% R&D credit rate and EMDG cap figures are accurate and well-hedged for this stage.

executive_summary.notes: Honestly flags competitor gap rather than fabricating names; outcome-based pricing point is useful but repeated in competitor_landscape.

service_providers.notes: Heavy repetition of providers already named in action_plan; NEXTGEN/Gadens/Dentons descriptions add little new decision-ready detail.

case_studies_guides.notes: All case-study links are internal relative URLs; Fenergo ANZ claims are plausible but unverified and presented without hedging.

competitor_landscape.notes: Admits no named competitors found yet fills section with generic positioning; fails the stated report focus of understanding competitors.

mentor_recommendations.notes: LinkedIn profiles cited but individuals' current roles and fintech relevance unverified; FinTech Australia and Investment NSW repeat prior sections.

investor_recommendations.notes: Angel check-size figures presented with false precision; Reinventure's Westpac link is historical and no longer active, unhedged.

lead_list.notes: No actual leads delivered; proposed lists are generic Melbourne HR/founder segments not tailored to boutique staffing agencies Floats actually targets.

action_plan.notes: Heavy repetition of R&DTI 43.5% offset advice and same providers (Fullstack, Stone & Chalk, H2) already covered in service_providers and setup_compliance sections.

swot_analysis.notes: Weaknesses section slightly generic; ATS integration friction point could name specific incumbent ATS vendors relevant to Australian boutique agencies.

first_customers.notes: Gadens is a law firm, not a recruitment buyer; mapping Floats as a resource-allocation tool for a law firm is a weak, implausible fit.

events_resources.notes: R&D documentation advice repeated from setup_compliance; CDAO Melbourne event URL and attendance figures not independently verifiable.

setup_compliance.notes: Superannuation rate cited as 11.5% but rate is 11.5% for FY2024 and rising; minor. R&DTI detail duplicated heavily across executive_summary and action_plan.

executive_summary.notes: Named real early clients and product features well; Go1 analogy repeated in action_plan. 'Who from your matches' section appears empty/truncated.

service_providers.notes: ARAIN and SMEC AI described as 'federal agency' and 'National AI Adopt Centre' interchangeably with unclear distinctions; several providers repeated verbatim from action_plan.

case_studies_guides.notes: Guide links appear to be internal platform URLs not independently verifiable; superannuation cited as 12% here vs 11.5% in setup_compliance — factual inconsistency.

competitor_landscape.notes: All competitors are sourcing-focused; no local Australian competitors (e.g. Expr3ss, Ascender) assessed, weakening completeness of local market picture.

mentor_recommendations.notes: Claire Marriott's relevance to R&D grant compliance is a stretch; LinkedIn profiles not verified as current roles. Navaid Khatib's fintech background weakly justified for HRTech AI.

investor_recommendations.notes: Smart50 is an awards program not a grant; Transition Level Investments has minimal verifiable public profile. No HRTech-specific VCs like Airtree or Blackbird mentioned.

lead_list.notes: ACL/AFSL filter for custom lists is a strong touch; dataset counts (260, 340) unverified but plausible and clearly labelled.

action_plan.notes: ANZ URL listed as 'anz.com.westpac.com' elsewhere bleeds in; minor but signals copy-paste error undermining grounding.

swot_analysis.notes: Real-time vs batch claim is specific but unverified; Stone & Chalk and accelerator opportunities repeat heavily from other sections.

first_customers.notes: ANZ URL is wrong ('anz.com.westpac.com'); 'Money Plan' procurement signal is unverified and presented as confirmed fact.

events_resources.notes: Specific 2026-2027 dates for named events are unverifiable and presented as confirmed; risks being entirely fabricated.

setup_compliance.notes: Strong grounding on A-UKFTA date, superannuation rate, R&D threshold; SC400 visa name is non-standard and should be hedged.

executive_summary.notes: Stone & Chalk and FinTech Australia accelerator pitch repeats verbatim from other sections; Contino case study link reused.

service_providers.notes: Stone & Chalk listed twice under different subheadings; NEXTGEN Group claim of 'Sales as a Service' is unverified.

case_studies_guides.notes: All case study links are internal slugs with no external verification; fabricated parallels presented as confirmed facts.

competitor_landscape.notes: Adrenalin Equity inclusion as a 'competitor' is irrelevant and confusing; Experian listed twice as separate entities inconsistently.

mentor_recommendations.notes: LinkedIn profiles cited for named individuals are unverified; Navaid Khatib's URL points to his own profile not AusPayNet.

investor_recommendations.notes: Angel investor check sizes and RegTech focus claims (Alistair Muir, Martyn Reeves) are unverified and presented as confirmed facts.

lead_list.notes: ANZ Trade dataset link feels generic; no named cleantech investors or WA-specific contacts despite that being the core ask.

action_plan.notes: NETA, Granton, Treadstone URLs unverified; R&D Tax Incentive advice repeated verbatim across multiple sections.

swot_analysis.notes: R&D Tax Incentive benefit duplicated from action_plan and setup_compliance; SWIS reference is strong local touch.

first_customers.notes: No named utility or government targets despite B2G focus; generic DNSP framing not specific to WA network operators.

events_resources.notes: Smart Energy 2027 URL implausible for a current event; pre-meeting investor tactics are generic, no WA-specific events listed.

setup_compliance.notes: Workers comp references icare/NSW and WorkSafe/Victoria despite company being Perth/WA-based; should cite WorkCover WA.

executive_summary.notes: Honest caveat on investor data is good, but R&D Tax Incentive and accelerator recommendations repeat content from other sections.

service_providers.notes: NEXTGEN Group inclusion feels irrelevant to cleantech seed stage; NETA $10k grant figure unverified and repeated from action_plan.

case_studies_guides.notes: Buildkite, Go1, Linktree are software companies with no cleantech relevance; case study links are internal placeholders not real sources.

competitor_landscape.notes: No named competitors despite claiming three tiers exist; SWIS duck curve mention is good but sovereign capability framing is generic.

mentor_recommendations.notes: LinkedIn URLs used as org URLs; Liza Noonan and Sam Cust roles unverifiable; Hyper Startup Studio appears fabricated.

investor_recommendations.notes: Mick Liubinskas check size of $25k stated with false precision; Croc Pitch is Darwin-based and weakly relevant to Perth cleantech.

lead_list.notes: Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital reference is plausible but Metro HHS specifics unverified; minor overlap with first_customers section.

action_plan.notes: Several named firms (LandedFlow, Treadstone, Stockwells) lack verifiable MedTech credentials; CCIWA is WA-focused, oddly placed. Repeats TGA advice from other sections.

swot_analysis.notes: Series A-B equated to '51-200 employees' without basis; SAFTA/RDTI claims are real but generic; threats repeat TGA timeline points stated elsewhere.

first_customers.notes: HSA comparable-regulator pathway claim is useful but overstated; QTenders and HHS specifics are accurate. Standing Offer Arrangements advice repeats action_plan.

events_resources.notes: ADX Brisbane is a dental event with no relevance to diagnostic MedTech; recommending it to vet medical device distributors is a significant grounding failure.

setup_compliance.notes: Director ID timeline and superannuation rate are accurate; SC400 visa name is incorrect (it is a subclass 400). Content is largely generic incorporation advice.

executive_summary.notes: 12-27 month TGA range is well-framed; 2032 Olympics hook is relevant. Slightly repeats entity-setup and distributor-parallel-pursuit advice found in action_plan.

service_providers.notes: LandedFlow described as distribution consultant but appears to be a different business; CCIWA is WA-specific and weakly justified. Repeats PharmOut/R2/Adjutor from action_plan.

case_studies_guides.notes: Secretlab, BYD, Kia, and Singtel are irrelevant sector comparisons for a MedTech regulatory entry; lessons are loosely analogised and undermine credibility.

competitor_landscape.notes: QHeart Medical link routes to a generic directory, not a verified company page; competitors chosen have minimal overlap with diagnostic imaging/AI diagnostics positioning.

mentor_recommendations.notes: Nigel Williams described as Clayton Utz advisor and ex-CBA CRO without verifiable connection to MedTech; LinkedIn URLs are generic and profiles unverified.

investor_recommendations.notes: Illumina for Startups grant reframed as Series A-B MedTech funding; it is genomics-specific and US$10k instrument access, not capital. Michal Wallas unverifiable.
